items.title: Squirrel Flower – “Not Me” & “Sick Tooth”
items.summary: Squirrel Flower is a longtime Stereogum favorite, and Ella Williams is arguably only getting better. "Reelin," the lead single from new album Say A Prayer To The Gods Of Getting Going, was a stunner. Williams has two more impressive previews of the new LP out today. The post Squirrel Flower – “Not Me” & “Sick Tooth” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Madi Diaz Announces Fatal Optimist (UN-unplugged) Feat. Lucy Dacus & August Ponthier: Hear “This is How A Woman Leaves”
items.summary: Last year, the great singer-songwriter Madi Diaz released her Fatal Optimist album. Later this summer, she'll expand on that record with the confusingly titled deluxe edition Fatal Optimist (UN-unplugged). The new version includes four new songs, including collaborations with Lucy Dacus and August Ponthier.

items.title: Heather The Jerk – “Write A Letter”
items.summary: Heather Sawyer has been in Madison punk bands like the Hussy, Proud Parents, and Boo/Hiss, and she makes her own spiky, gnarly, catchy DIY tracks as Heather The Jerk.

items.title: 2026 Emmy Nominations: ‘Widow’s Bay,’ ‘Pluribus,’ ‘Hacks,’ ‘The Pitt,’ more
items.summary: HBO’s The Pitt and Hacks led the pack with 25 and 24 nominations each, followed by Widow’s Bay (19), Pluribus (18), Beef S2 (16) & DTF St Louis (13).

items.title: Cortex announce fall tour with Adrian Younge and J Rocc
items.summary: The much-sampled psychedelic French jazz-funk legends will be heading back to the US and Canada in September and October.

items.title: WAVVES & Say Anything announce new collab LP ‘Cherry Soda’ (new single & exclusive vinyl)
items.summary: ”He became more emo and I became more Wavves-y,” Max Bemis says of working with Nathan Williams.

10 Celebrities Who’ve Changed Their Lives with Ayahuasca
items.summary: In 2016, Chelsea Handler filmed her ayahuasca ceremony for the Netflix docuseries Chelsea Does. In the decade that’s followed, psychedelic healing has continued to bleed into the mainstream, from pop culture to TV, film, and music. items.title: King Gizzard Dances With The ‘Alien’ On New LP
items.summary: King Gizzard and the Lizard Wizard have put a name and a face to their long-awaited next foray into electronic music.

items.title: Lucy Dacus Celebrates Richmond In Self-Directed Video
items.summary: Shot entirely in her hometown of Richmond, Va., the video doubles as a love letter to the city that shaped her.
